Validation evidence - query, response, timestamp, user context - is logged against each pattern. Patterns not executed in N days are flagged for review, not deleted.

The prototype

Running on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry: Next.js application, SAP AppRouter for XSUAA authentication, PostgreSQL knowledge graph, BTP Destination Service for OData connectivity.

 

Context: SAP's official path for custom agents connecting to Joule is the Agent Gateway - documented in the SAP Architecture Center as exposing Joule Agents via A2A 0.3.0 with IAS App2App authentication. The Agent Gateway is not yet generally available. This prototype takes the developer path in the interim: a self-hosted A2A endpoint on BTP CF using XSUAA JWT. When the Agent Gateway reaches GA, the authentication model and registration process would change - the KG and OData runtime layer underneath would not.

Fig 4. * Service token today; principal propagation (user identity through to OData) is on the roadmap. When Agent Gateway reaches GA, the entry point shifts from self-hosted AppRouter to SAP-managed endpoint - the KG and OData layers remain unchanged.

Dual protocol handler

The endpoint accepts both A2A JSON-RPC 2.0 and Joule's native agent-request format. Both converge on the same resolver:

 

The resolver: KG lookup, slot extraction, OData, render

 

The knowledge graph schema

Joule's contextId field carries pagination state across conversation turns. When a user says "show more," the skip offset and pattern ID are encoded in the context ID,  so the next turn re-runs the same OData query from the correct offset without a new KG lookup.

Closing thoughts

The pieces fit together reasonably well. The A2A protocol is documented and implemented in Joule. The OData catalog is rich and well-governed. BTP handles authentication and connectivity. The engineering gaps - vector search, slot resolution, principal propagation are real but well-defined.

For teams building on Joule and A2A today, this suggests a possible shift in focus: from defining individual capabilities to curating reusable, validated access patterns. The two approaches are not mutually exclusive ; capability builds are what populate the KG in the first place. But the longer-term value may compound differently when patterns are shared rather than siloed per use case.

The idea to sit with

This is not a replacement for Joule capabilities. It is a complementary layer - one that may make them more scalable over time.

The real question is not can this work?
It is: who owns the knowledge graph?

One possible direction for SAP AI is not simply more capabilities, but fewer, more adaptive ones backed by a shared knowledge layer that agents draw on at runtime. SAP is delivering the ingredients: Joule as orchestrator, A2A as the routing standard, BDC as the semantic foundation.

Who else is exploring this direction?

This post reflects a personal exploration and learning exercise building on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry. All views are my own and do not represent SAP's positions, roadmap, or product direction. Code snippets are from a personal prototype and are shared for illustrative purposes only.

Co-Authored with J4C and Sonnet 🙂

entry.title: SAP Business AI in banking and insurance - Partner live expert session
entry.content:
Banking and insurance clients are asking sharper questions about AI.  They want results, not theory.  This session helps you answer with confidence.

Join our live expert session on SAP Business AI for banking and insurance.  You will get a clear view of market trends and where AI is already creating impact.  We will walk through real use cases, show how to position AI in conversations with clients, and share who to engage when you need support.

Plus, you will see a live demo on SAP Demo Environment 2.0.  It is always easier to sell when you can show, not tell.

The thing is, partners who move early in AI conversations build stronger trust.  This is a simple way to get there.

Register now and bring practical AI stories into your next customer meeting.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/apr/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013878.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #BankingAI #InsuranceInnovation #BusinessAI

entry.title: SAP Developer News for April 23rd, 2026
entry.content:
ITEMS 

Migration tool for your SAP Build Apps frontend web applications 

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/migration-tool-for-your-sap-build-apps-frontend-web-applications/ba-p/14368805  
  • SAP Help page https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/sap-build-apps-path-forward#migrating-from-the-sap-build-apps-standalone-offering-to-sap-build 

SAP Open Source Webinar: Olympic Sailing on Eclipse Technology – From Closed Source to Open Source 

  • Event info https://events.sap.com/olympic-sailing-on-eclipse-technology-from-closed-source-to-open/en_us/home.html  

SAP BTP Platform Automation Roundtable #3 

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/customer-amp-partner-roundtable-for-sap-btp-platform-automation-3-april/ba-p/14374109  

DYK #13 Override default auth while using xsuaa (CAP) 

  • Capire: Authentication in Production – Enforced by Default https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/authentication#enforced-by-default  

CHAPTER TITLES 

00:00 Intro 
00:07 Migration tool for your SAP Build Apps frontend web applications 
01:31 SAP Open Source Webinar: Olympic Sailing on Eclipse Technology – From Closed Source to Open Source 
02:24 SAP BTP Platform Automation Roundtable #3 
03:24 DYK #13 Override default auth while using xsuaa (CAP) 

TRANSCRIPT

[Intro]
This is the SAP Developer News for April the 23rd, 2026.

[Shrini]
Hello, SAP community. By now, you may have seen the announcement about SAP Build Apps deprecation as of March 2026. Understandably, the first question on everyone's mind is, what happens to the apps I am already building? The team has been listening closely to the feedback from the community. To make this transition as seamless as possible, SAP has rolled out a migration tool tool for your SAP Build Apps projects. This tool will transform your existing front-end web applications into a fully structured React or SAP UI5 project. Once transformed, you can easily continue enhancing them within your SAP Build environment using Business Application Studio. If you are ready to migrate, you can reach out to SAP by opening a support ticket with the mtar file of your project, specify whether the app should be based on React or SAPUI5 and authorize the transformation. Detailed instructions on creating this ticket are available on this help page. The link can be found in the description. This tool was built based on your feedback. Let us know how this works for you. Finally, a quick reminder, you will continue to have full access to SAP BuildApps for the entire duration of your current contract.

[Mamikee]
Sailing Analytics is now open source, and if you want to learn more about the journey behind it, there's an upcoming webinar you don't want to miss. On May 20th, 2026 at 3pm CEST, join the technical webinar on Eclipse Azimuth Sailing Analytics, a real-time regatta tracking and analysis tool that has been used at more than 1,000 sailing events, including the Olympic Games. In the session, product owner Axel will walk through SAP's journey to open source the solution, including licensing strategy, internal processing, collaboration with the Eclipse Foundation, and community building efforts. Now, if you're interested in open source, real-world analytics solutions, or how projects like this evolve beyond SAP, this is definitely one to check out. You can learn more and register in the link below.

[DJ]
Are you interested in the Terraform and CLI tools for managing your BTP resources? Sharing opinions and experiences on this topic with others? Then the BTP Platform Automation Customer and Partner Roundtable series is for you. And the third instance of the series is just around the corner. It's on Tuesday, the 12th of May at As usual, there will be some info on latest updates, a round of Q&A, some demos and more. There's a blog
post with further details, a link that you can use to register. It's free, by the way. And also in that blog post, there's a link to content from the previous two instances. See you there.

[Shrini]
Did you know you can override the default authentication behavior for CAP service endpoints using a simple feature flag? By default, when you deploy a CAP service to Cloud Foundry with a production -grade authentication strategy, all service endpoints are protected. This happens because CAP implicitly adds the @requires authenticated user annotation to every service. But what if you want to make some endpoints publicly accessible, especially during testing or for specific use cases? You can disable this default behavior by adding the flag RestrictAllServicesToFalse to your package.json. In this batch request service, setting the RestrictAllServices flag to false makes all service endpoints publicly accessible by default, meaning no authentication is required. However, the createBatch action is explicitly protected using the @RequiresAuthenticatedUser annotation, ensuring that only authenticated users can invoke it.

entry.title: Managing Content Change Notification Issues During SAP Fiori Launchpad Content Exposure to SAP BTP
entry.content:
With the SAP Fiori launchpad content exposure, you can integrate launchpad content from a product into SAP BTP by using an automatic synchronization. During the automatic synchronization, it may happen that the exposure log receives the following error message “Notification for content channel ‘xyz’ not sent. Manual fetch required” (Message Class: /UI2/CDM3_EXP, Message Number: 399).

As you may not immediately recognize that this issue happened, this blog post provides a way of how you can monitor this type of error message using email notification from SAP Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (SAP Cloud ALM).

In short, in the SAP Cloud ALM Integration & Exception Monitoring app, you do the following:

  1. Enable the ABAP application log for the system          
  2. Add a filter for the error message
  3. Add a notification for the error

As an example, we use an SAP S/4HANA system.

Disclaimer: This article is written from the viewpoint of a consumer of SAP Cloud ALM. If you have SAP Cloud ALM-specific questions, such as how to set up your system in SAP Cloud ALM, please reach out to SAP Cloud ALM directly.

Check the following SAP Help Portal pages to see whether you are affected by this issue:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • Enable Content Change Notifications
  • Display Launchpad Content Exposure Logs

SAP Integrated Business Planning

  • Enable Content Change Notifications
  • Display Launchpad Content Exposure Logs

SAP S/4HANA on-premise

  • Manage Content Change Notifications
  • Launchpad Content Exposure Troubleshooting - Error Analysis   

Prerequisites

Before we can start with the parts specific to launchpad exposure in the SAP Cloud ALM configuration, you set up your system in SAP Cloud ALM.

In addition, please ensure that you have the Integration Architect role. See SAP Cloud ALM overview page.

Enabling ABAP Application Log for Your System

First, you configure your system to collect data for the ABAP Application Log.

  1. In SAP Cloud ALM, in the Operations space, open the Integration & Exception Monitoring app.
  2. Now, let’s add a system tile to the overview: In the upper right corner, choose the Select a Scope icon and filter for your system. Select it in the table and choose Apply.
  3. On the overview page, choose the system tile and from its menu, choose Edit Configuration.
  4. For your system, toggle the Data Collection to On.
  5. For your system, choose the arrow icon to go to the details page.
  6. Activate the ABAP Application Log entry.

Adding a Filter for the Launchpad Content Exposure Error Message

Now, let’s create a filter so that we can monitor when the error message “Notification for content channel ‘xyz’ not sent. Manual fetch required” is written to the ABAP Application Log.

  1. In the same screen as above, for the ABAP Application Log, enter the monitoring and events view.
  2. To add the filter, in the Filters table, choose Add.
  3. Enter a filter name. Then, enter the following filter parameters:

    Parameter

    Operator

    Value

    Object

    Is

    /UI2/BE

    Subobject

    Is

    /UI2/CDM3

    Message ID

    Is

    /UI2/CDM3_EXP

    Message Number

    Is

    399

    Status

    Is

    E


  4. Save the filter by choosing the Save button at the bottom of the dialog.

Adding an Event to Be Notified About an Error

The last step is to create an event so that you get notified once the filter which we have created in the previous step detects the launchpad content exposure error message.

Please note that this event will not only be initiated for the launchpad content exposure error message. If you had configured other ABAP Application Log filters in addition, the event will be initiated as well. The reason is that on the date of publication of this blog post, according to 3452860 - How to setup filters for the category "ABAP application log" in Exception Monitoring of SAP Cloud ALM, it is not possible to add filters to the event itself.

  1. To create the event, navigate to the Events tab and choose Add.
  2. As Event Name, choose Erroneous ABAP Application Log and enter a display name.
  3. Define the actions: Activate Create Alert. For example, you can also configure the event to send an email once there is an error written into the ABAP Application Log.
     
  4. Save your entries.

Checking the Event

In the Integration & Exception Monitoring app, you can view all events that you have created and that were initiated on the Alerting tab.

  1. Choose the Alerting tab.
  2. Once the launchpad content exposure error happens, you can see it in the table

Conclusion:

This blog post covered how to proactively monitor HTTP 401 content change notification failures during SAP Fiori launchpad content exposure to SAP BTP using SAP Cloud ALM's Integration & Exception Monitoring app. You have learned how to enable ABAP Application Log data collection, create a targeted filter for message /UI2/CDM3_EXP 399, and configure automated email notifications to detect synchronization issues before they impact your users. By implementing this monitoring approach, you can transform reactive troubleshooting into proactive incident management, ensuring continuous content availability between your on-premise systems and SAP BTP.

If you have questions about the configuration steps or would like to share your experiences with launchpad content exposure monitoring, please feel free to leave a comment below.

entry.title: Build Solutions for SAP Cloud ERP with confidence
entry.content:
Partner Reference Application Family provides Partners with a “golden path” to becoming a SaaS provider of multi-tenant applications based on the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). The guidance covers building, running, and integrating scalable full-stack cloud applications. Whether you are building a brand-new SaaS product or upgrading an existing one, these resources act as a blueprint for success.

It provides ready-to-run code branches and tutorials to accelerate the development of scalable SaaS solutions. It consists of multiple public SAP-samples repositories on GitHub, offering comprehensive guidance aligned with the “principle of one” and “clean core”. These resources highlight the value of the extensibility environment, demonstrate best practices in code quality and architecture, and serve as demo-ready examples for realistic partner extensions.

CAP Partner Reference Application
A GitHub repository featuring the sample multi-tenant solution “Poetry Slam Manager” built on SAP BTP CAP, along with detailed tutorials for developers and application managers.

When delivering standardized SaaS solutions to multiple customers, customer-specific requirements are inevitable. Using SAP Build and CAP, the solution is extensible out of the box, enabling easy customization. The companion repository, "Partner Reference Application Extension", provides guidance on building and deploying such extensions, including a sample customer-specific extension (“Catering Management”).

ABAP Partner Reference Application

For building ABAP-based side-by-side multi-tenant applications for SAP Cloud ERP, refer to the end-to-end guide. It includes the sample solution “Music Festival Manager” based on ABAP Cloud.

Cross Stack Partner Reference Application
Designed for scenarios requiring on-stack extensions on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with seamless integration to SAP BTP CAP applications.
This example demonstrates how an on-stack extension integrates with a side-by-side CAP multi-tenant application (Poetry Slam Manager). It includes tutorials to help build and integrate both components, enabling enterprise-grade cross-stack extensions using ABAP Cloud and CAP.

On-Stack Partner Reference Application
Focused on delivering on-stack extensions for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with scalability, upgrade stability, and seamless integration.
This application integrates with Manage Sales Orders – Version 2 (SAP Fiori app) and showcases event-driven loyalty program management. It demonstrates how standard SAP business processes can be enhanced without modifying the core. Top of Form

 

The Partner Reference Application Family is your accelerator for building next-generation SAP solutions. It’s not just about code—it’s about enabling partners to deliver scalable, extensible, and future-proof applications with speed and quality. By leveraging these ready-made blueprints and best practices, you can reduce development effort, ensure clean core compliance, and focus on what truly matters: delivering value to your customers.

entry.title: New Learning Journey: Implementing Joule Across Your Organizational System Landscape
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the new Learning Journey, "Implementing Joule Across Your Organizational System Landscape." This free, comprehensive program is tailored for consultants looking to lead SAP customers through the adoption and optimization of Joule, SAP’s AI solution for intelligent, connected workflows.

The journey includes step-by-step resources, from foundational knowledge of the Joule ecosystem to practical guidance on activating and customizing Joule in SAP SuccessFactors, SAP S/4HANA, and more. You will also learn how to enable advanced Joule features—such as document grounding and analytical insights—and extend its capabilities to non-SAP environments, all while maintaining robust governance and security.

As a consultant, you will gain expert-level, up-to-date knowledge, empowering you to deliver tangible business value to your clients.

 

Key Takeaways:

  • Understand Joule’s ecosystem, architecture, and integration across SAP and non-SAP systems.
  • Learn to activate, configure, and customize Joule for SAP SuccessFactors, SAP S/4HANA, SAP Ariba, SAP Concur, and SAP Signavio.
  • Enable advanced features like document grounding and analytics in the SAP landscape.
  • Troubleshoot common setup issues and stay current with Joule updates and resources.
  • Extend Joule’s value to experience platforms and external environments securely.

Start Learning: Implementing Joule Across your Organizational System Landscape

entry.title: Destination Anywhere — A VS Code REST Client for SAP BTP Developers
entry.content:
Destination Anywhere

The REST client for SAP BTP developers.
Send HTTP and OData requests straight from VS Code — with zero-config destination resolution.

  • dest:// for BTP subaccount destinations
  • mdk:// for Mobile Services app destinations
  • Variables and environments
  • Sidebar browser
  • On-premise support

Why Destination Anywhere?

You're coding in VS Code, you need to test an API call against your BTP backend, and suddenly you're out of your editor — opening the BTP cockpit to look up a destination URL, switching to another tool to craft the request, grabbing a bearer token from somewhere, and forget about reaching on-premise systems that are only accessible from SAP BTP via Cloud Connector. Every round-trip breaks your flow.

Destination Anywhere keeps you where you already are. Type dest://MY_DESTINATION/path in a .dest file, press Cmd+Alt+R, and the extension resolves the real URL, handles authentication, and shows you the response — without ever leaving VS Code.

No cockpit tab. No manual tokens. No pasting URLs you looked up somewhere else. Just your editor.

Write a request, click Send, get the response. That's it.

From Install to First Request

Here's what happens the first time you open the extension — no setup guide needed, the sidebar walks you through it.

1. You're not logged in yet

Open the Destination Anywhere panel in the sidebar. If you're not logged in to Cloud Foundry, the extension tells you immediately and offers a one-click login button.

The sidebar detects your CF login state automatically.

2. Log in without leaving VS Code

Click Login with CF Tools and the CF sign-in form opens right inside VS Code. Enter your SSO passcode or credentials — no terminal needed.

CF Tools sign-in, right inside VS Code. SSO passcode or credentials — your choice.

3. No Destination Service? No problem.

Don't have a Destination Service instance? Most SAP BTP projects already have one — if yours does, the extension reuses it automatically and you'll never see this step. If not, here's why you need one: Destinations in SAP BTP are configured at the subaccount level in the BTP cockpit — they exist independently of any service instance. But the only way to read those destinations programmatically is through the Destination Service REST API. That API requires OAuth2 credentials (clientid, clientsecret, uri) which you can only obtain from a Destination Service instance and its service key in your CF space. So even though you never need an instance to create destinations, SAP BTP requires one just to query them. No instance, no credentials, no API access.

Full transparency — this extension and the router app are both open source. When you use on-premise destinations, Destination Anywhere deploys a small Express app to your CF space to relay requests through Cloud Connector. The entire source code — both the extension and the router app — is right here in this repository. The router is a single-file Express server (~100 lines) under router-app/ that you can read, audit, and even deploy manually yourself if you prefer.

Manual deployment is simple — go to the router-app folder, edit manifest.yml with your service instance names, then run cf push. See router-app/README.md for full instructions. Nothing hidden, nothing proprietary — you're always in control of what runs in your CF space.

When you open the sidebar, the extension scans your CF space for any existing destination service instance. If you already have one — from another project, a managed app router, or manual setup — it reuses that. Nothing is created, nothing changes.

If no instance exists, the sidebar shows a "Create Destination Service instance..." link. Click it and the extension creates a lite plan instance named dest-anywhere-dest with a service key dest-anywhere-dest-key. This is free, one-time, and persists across sessions.

No Destination Service instance in this space — click to create one on the free lite plan.

Auto-provisioning in action: the extension creates a service instance, generates keys, and loads your destinations.

4. Browse your destinations

After provisioning, your BTP destinations appear in the sidebar tree. Each destination shows its type (Internet / OnPremise) and authentication method. Right-click any destination to insert a sample request into your .dest file or view its full configuration.

Right-click a destination: insert a ready-to-send request or inspect its properties.

One click inserts a complete request block with the right URL and headers.

5. Inspect destination details

Want to see what a destination resolves to? Click Destination Info and a panel opens showing the resolved URL, authentication type, proxy settings, and all configuration properties — with sensitive values masked.

Full destination properties at a glance — URL, auth type, token endpoint, and more.

Mobile Services (mdk://)

Destination Anywhere also supports SAP Mobile Services destinations via the mdk:// protocol. The first time you use it, the extension opens your browser for SAP BTP authentication — no extra confirmation needed.

Your browser opens to the SAP BTP login page. Once authenticated, the sidebar shows your Mobile Services apps and their destinations alongside BTP destinations. Use mdk://AppId/DestName/path to send requests:

GET mdk://SAM2405/DEST_SAM2405_PPROP/MyEquipments?$top=10&$format=json
Accept: application/json

Mobile Services authenticated. Both dest:// and mdk:// requests in a single .dest file.

Usage Examples

BTP Destinations (dest://)

@SYSTEM = MY_S4_SYSTEM
@service = /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER

### Metadata
GET dest://{{system}}{{service}}/$metadata
Accept: application/xml

### Business partners with filter + expand
GET dest://{{system}}{{service}}/A_BusinessPartner?$filter=BusinessPartnerCategory eq '1'&$expand=to_BusinessPartnerAddress&$top=5&$format=json
Accept: application/json

### Create a business partner
POST dest://{{system}}{{service}}/A_BusinessPartner
Content-Type: application/json

{"BusinessPartnerCategory": "1", "BusinessPartnerFullName": "Acme Corp"}

Mobile Services Destinations (mdk://)

### Equipment list via MDK
GET mdk://SAM2405.SAM.WIN/DEST_SAM2405_PPROP/MyEquipments?$top=10&$format=json
Accept: application/json

### Create a notification
POST mdk://SAM2405.SAM.WIN/DEST_SAM2405_PPROP/Notifications
Content-Type: application/json

{"NotificationType": "PM", "Description": "Equipment failure reported via MDK"}

Plain HTTP (no BTP)

@Host = https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com

### Get a post
GET {{host}}/posts/1

### Create a post
POST {{host}}/posts
Content-Type: application/json

{"title": "Hello from Destination Anywhere", "body": "Works with any HTTP endpoint.", "userId": 1}

Variables and Environments

Define @Var = value inline, use .env files, or configure per-environment variable sets in VS Code settings:

"destinationAnywhere.environmentVariables":
  "dev": { "system": "MY_DEV_SYSTEM", "base": "/sap/opu/odata/sap" }
  "prod": { "system": "MY_PROD_SYSTEM", "base": "/sap/opu/odata/sap" }

Then in your .dest file:

### Switches based on active environment
GET dest://{{system}}{{base}}/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartner?$top=5

Switch via Command Palette → Destination Anywhere: Switch Environment.

On-Premise Destinations

On-premise destinations route through SAP Cloud Connector, which is only reachable from inside Cloud Foundry. Destination Anywhere gives you two options:

Router app (recommended): One-click deploy of a lightweight Express app to your CF space. It uses SAP Cloud SDK to tunnel requests through Cloud Connector. XSUAA authentication is enforced. Use this when you have no local Cloud Connector or VPN. View the source code.

Direct mode: Sends requests directly to the destination URL using resolved credentials. Use this when a local Cloud Connector or VPN can reach the backend.

Deploy the router from the sidebar: expand BTP Destinations, and click Deploy On-Premise Router. The extension provisions Connectivity and XSUAA service instances automatically.

Key Features

  • dest:// protocol — Route requests through SAP BTP subaccount destinations. The extension resolves the real URL and injects authentication headers (Basic, OAuth2, SAML, Principal Propagation) automatically.
  • mdk:// protocol — Send requests through SAP Mobile Services App Router. Authenticate once via browser, then fire OData requests against MDK app destinations.
  • Sidebar panel — Browse all BTP destinations and Mobile Services apps in a tree view. Right-click to insert a sample request or view destination details.
  • On-premise routing — Access on-premise systems behind Cloud Connector. Deploy a lightweight router app to your CF space with one click — or use direct mode with a local Cloud Connector / VPN.
  • Variables and environments — Define @Var = value in your file, use .env files, or configure per-environment variable sets in settings. Switch environments from the Command Palette.
  • Syntax highlighting — Dedicated .dest language with a TextMate grammar — methods, URLs, dest:// / mdk:// schemes, headers, variables, and JSON bodies are all highlighted.
  • CodeLens — Click "Send Request" above any request block. Or press Cmd+Alt+R.
  • Smart autocomplete — HTTP methods, common headers, content types, and dest:// / mdk:// URL snippets.
  • Response panel — Status code (color-coded), response time, size, headers table, and pretty-printed body with JSON syntax highlighting and XML indentation. Copy body to clipboard in one click.
  • CF guard — Detects CF CLI installation and login state before making any BTP calls. Offers one-click login via CF Tools or a terminal fallback. Log out from the sidebar title bar.
  • Auto-provisioning — No Destination Service instance? The extension creates one for you (lite plan). Service keys are created and rotated as needed.
  • Caching — Resolved destinations, service credentials, and OAuth tokens are cached with configurable TTL. Clear everything with one command.

Commands

All commands are available in the Command Palette (Cmd+Shift+P / Ctrl+Shift+P😞

  • Destination Anywhere: Send Request — Send the request under the cursor
  • Destination Anywhere: Switch Environment — Change the active variable environment
  • Destination Anywhere: Clear Destination Cache — Purge all cached destinations, credentials, and tokens
  • Destination Anywhere: Login to Cloud Foundry — Open CF login via CF Tools or terminal
  • Destination Anywhere: Logout from Cloud Foundry — Log out from CF and clear all cached credentials
  • Destination Anywhere: Login to Mobile Services — Authenticate with SAP Mobile Services via browser
  • Destination Anywhere: Open Destinations Panel — Focus the sidebar tree view
  • Destination Anywhere: Create Destination Service instance — Create a Destination Service lite instance in the current CF space
  • Destination Anywhere: Deploy On-Premise Router — Deploy the Express-based router app for on-premise connectivity
  • Destination Anywhere: Uninstall On-Premise Router — Remove the router app and its service instances

Keyboard shortcut: Cmd+Alt+R / Ctrl+Alt+R → Send Request

Configuration

  • destinationAnywhere.timeout (default: 30000) — Request timeout in milliseconds
  • destinationAnywhere.followRedirects (default: true) — Follow HTTP redirects
  • destinationAnywhere.rejectUnauthorized (default: true) — Reject unauthorized SSL certificates
  • destinationAnywhere.defaultHeaders (default: {}) — Headers included in every request
  • destinationAnywhere.environmentVariables (default: {}) — Per-environment variable sets
  • destinationAnywhere.activeEnvironment (default: "") — Currently active environment
  • destinationAnywhere.destinationServiceUrl (default: "") — Manual Destination Service URL override (auto-discovered by default)
  • destinationAnywhere.mobileServicesUrl (default: "") — Manual Mobile Services URL override
  • destinationAnywhere.destinationCacheTTL (default: 300) — Destination cache TTL in seconds
  • destinationAnywhere.logLevel (default: "info") — Log level: off, error, warn, info, debug

Requirements

  • VS Code 1.87+
  • CF CLI — for dest:// and mdk:// features (install guide)
  • SAP BTP subaccount

Plain HTTP requests (https://...) work without CF CLI or any SAP services.

Installation

Install from the Visual Studio Code Marketplace:

  1. Open VS Code
  2. Press Ctrl+Shift+X (Windows/Linux) or Cmd+Shift+X (macOS) to open the Extensions view
  3. Search for Destination Anywhere
  4. Click Install

Or install from the command line: code --install-extension aydin-ozcan.destination-anywhere
